title: Future vs IO semantics
description: scala.concurrent.Future is eager and memoized; Cats-Effect IO is a lazy, referentially transparent description run on demand.
body: Use when choosing or reasoning about an async type. A Future begins executing the moment it is created and caches its single result, so it is not referentially transparent (val f = Future(sideEffect) already ran) and cannot be re-run or easily cancelled. An IO/Task is a lazy value describing an effect: building it does nothing, it can be composed, retried, and cancelled, and runs only when interpreted at the edge. Do not treat a Future as a re-runnable recipe or assume IO has executed just because it was constructed. Actions: pick IO for principled effect control, Future for interop with libraries that demand it, wrapping construction to delay it. Validation signals include a test distinguishing construction from execution. Risk boundary: eager Future side effects fire before composition, causing duplicate or out-of-order effects.
